title: Slow-cooker beef stew
subtitle: British beef and vegetable stew
excerpt: Beef chuck braised all day in the slow cooker with carrots, potato, thyme, and beef stock until the meat is falling apart and the vegetables are soft. A proper winter stew with no attention required after the morning. Serves four.
sourceNotes: British beef stew is a working-class staple built on the French braise: slow cooking of a tough cut in liquid until the collagen breaks down to gelatin and the sauce thickens naturally. The slow cooker reproduces the long braise at a lower temperature, typically around 90°C on LOW, which is below the boil and gentler on the meat than an oven. Documented across twentieth century British cookery from Marguerite Patten to Jane Grigson.

content.text: The slow cooker does not replace the browning step. Colour on the beef and the onions contributes flavour that low-and-slow heat alone cannot produce. Spend 15 minutes at the hob getting everything properly browned before it goes in the slow cooker, and the stew will be noticeably better. The flour in the recipe coats the beef and thickens the sauce as it cooks.

Toss the beef chunks in {{plain-flour}} of flour, seasoned with {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt and {{black-pepper}} of pepper. Heat {{olive-oil}} of olive oil in a wide frying pan over high heat. Brown the beef in batches for 3 to 4 minutes per batch until well coloured. Transfer to the slow cooker. In the same pan, fry the {{onion}} of chopped onion over medium heat for 5 minutes until softened. Add {{garlic}} of garlic and {{tomato-puree}} of tomato purée; stir for 1 minute. Pour over {{stock-beef}} of stock and bring to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits. Pour over the beef in the slow cooker. Add the carrots, potatoes, and thyme sprigs. Cover and cook on LOW for 7 to 8 hours or HIGH for 4 to 5 hours until the beef is falling apart and the vegetables are tender. Remove the thyme stems and taste for seasoning. Serve with crusty bread.

items.fix: Ladle the stew liquid into a small saucepan and reduce over high heat for 5 to 10 minutes until thickened. Return to the slow cooker and stir through.
items.cause: Slow cooker releases liquid from the vegetables and meat as it cooks, diluting the stock.

items.fix: Chuck or braising steak is the only right cut for this. Lean beef will not soften. Check that the slow cooker reaches a simmer on LOW, some older models run hotter than others.
items.cause: Wrong cut, or slow cooker running too cool.

items.fix: Cut potatoes into larger chunks (4 cm) and add them halfway through cooking rather than at the start. Waxy varieties hold up better than floury ones.
items.cause: Cut too small, or cooked too long.

content.text: LOW is the better setting for this stew. The 8-hour cook at around 90°C breaks down the collagen in the beef chuck more gently than the rapid simmer of HIGH. The result on LOW is a more gelatinous sauce and meat that tears apart rather than shredding into fibres. HIGH works if time is short, but the texture is noticeably different.
